steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the function of the underlined lines: 'The bleak fields are asleep, / My heart alone wakes.' The key word is 'alone' — while everything in the evening world rests, the speaker is awake in solitude. The answer must capture what these lines do within the poem as a whole.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — It conveys the speaker's sense that he is alone while experiencing the evening.: directly captures the contrast between the sleeping world and the speaker's solitary wakefulness — ✓
  • Choice B — It shows that the speaker would rather look at the fields than at the water.: no such preference appears in the underlined lines or anywhere in the poem — ✗
  • Choice C — It indicates the speaker's disappointment that other people dislike rural settings.: other people are never mentioned in the poem — ✗
  • Choice D — It explains why the speaker has woken up from sleeping.: reverses the relationship — the speaker is awake while the world sleeps, not waking from sleep — ✗
That confirms A is correct.
